  "english": {
    "IsA": [
      "It is a cross and circle board game."
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "It has wooden pawns.",
      "It has seven cowry shells.",
      "It has four men or pieces called Sogtha.",
      "It has a home column for each player.",
      "It has a starting point which is a flower motif.",
      "It has safe squares called Cheere.",
      "It has a total of 8 safe squares."
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "It is characterized by variations played throughout India.",
      "It is characterized by a bantering manner of play.",
      "It is characterized by the need to knock out at least one man of another player before bringing men home.",
      "It is characterized by the requirement for an exact number of steps to enter the center cross home.",
      "It is characterized by the possibility of games never ending due to specific moves.",
      "It is characterized by the prohibition of fixing cowry shells before throwing."
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "It is used for determining each player's move.",
      "It is used for playing a game.",
      "It is used for distracting an opponent with details, stories and tales.",
      "It is used for moving pieces around without the opponent noticing.",
      "It is used for entering men into the game.",
      "It is used for moving men around the outer perimeter columns.",
      "It is used for knocking out at least one man of another player.",
      "It is used for bringing men home.",
      "It is used for removing a pawn voluntarily by playing a specific number.",
      "It is used for aiding a partner thereafter."
    ],

      "It is located in India."
    ],

    "Affects": [
      "It affects the opponent's turn.",
      "It affects the movement of pieces.",
      "It affects the outcome of the game."
    ],
    "ConsistsOf": [
      "It consists of a board made of wool or cloth.",
      "It consists of wooden pawns.",
      "It consists of seven cowry shells.",
      "It consists of three four-sided long dice in some variations.",
      "It consists of an embroidered cloth in the shape of a cross.",
      "It consists of columns and squares on the board.",
      "It consists of pieces called Sogtha.",
      "It consists of four men per player, although variants exist using 8 men each.",
      "It consists of a center cross which is home.",
      "It consists of a home column for each player's men."
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "It is part of games similar to pachisi.",
      "It is part of variations played throughout India.",
      "It is part of games identified from Iron Age.",
      "It is part of the epic poem Mahabharata.",
      "It is part of the game of dice.",
      "It is part of derivations including Gyan chauper and Ludo."
    ],
    "MemberOf": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "It is known for being very similar to pachisi.",
      "It is known for its variations played throughout India.",
      "It is known for its unique playing style.",
      "It is known for the concept of losing with a bay-thoree.",
      "It is known for the specific rules regarding entering men and moving them.",
      "It is known for the scoring system based on cowry shells.",
      "It is known for the safe squares called Cheere.",
      "It is known for the strategy of knocking out opponents' men.",
      "It is known for the objective of bringing all men home to win.",
      "It is known for the possibility of games lasting many hours."
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "It is associated with India.",
      "It is associated with the Iron Age.",
      "It is associated with the epic poem Mahabharata.",
      "It is associated with Yudhishthira and Duryodhan.",
      "It is associated with kings who played this game.",
      "It is associated with trained mice called Sundari and Mundari.",
      "It is associated with bantering and mocking play.",
      "It is associated with specific terms like 'ghar', 'thore', 'tohd', 'cheere', 'beli jaye', 'peghedu', 'bandhana', 'charkoni', 'alkhee'."
    ],
    "NamedAfter": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "OccurredIn": [

      "It occurred in the epic poem Mahabharata."

      "It is about the different variations and strategies used in the game.",
      "It is about the scoring system and winning conditions."
    ],

      "It was influenced by the game of dice played in the epic poem Mahabharata."

      "It influenced Ludo."

  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それはクロスとサークル型のボードゲームです。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それは木製の駒を持っています。",
      "それは7つのカウリ貝を持っています。",
      "それはソグタと呼ばれる4つの駒またはピースを持っています。",
      "それは各プレイヤーのホームカラムを持っています。",
      "それは花のモチーフであるスタート地点を持っています。",
      "それはチーレと呼ばれるセーフスクエアを持っています。",
      "それは合計8つのセーフスクエアを持っています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それはインド全土でプレイされるバリエーションによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはからかい合いながらプレイするスタイルによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはホームに戻る前に少なくとも1人の対戦相手の駒をノックアウトする必要があることによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはホームに入るために正確な数のステップが必要であることによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは特定のムーブによってゲームが決して終わらない可能性があることによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはカウリ貝を投げる前に固定することが禁止されていることによって特徴づけられます。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それは各プレイヤーの動きを決定するために使用されます。",
      "それはゲームをプレイするために使用されます。",
      "それは相手を詳細、物語、話で気を散らすために使用されます。",
      "それは相手に気づかれずに駒を動かすために使用されます。",
      "それは駒をゲームに入れるために使用されます。",
      "それは外周の列を駒が移動するために使用されます。",
      "それは少なくとも1人の対戦相手の駒をノックアウトするために使用されます。",
      "それは駒をホームに戻すために使用されます。",
      "それは特定の数でプレイすることによってポーンを削除するために使用されます。",
      "それは後でパートナーを助けるために使用されます。"
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "それはインドにあります。"
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "不明"
    ],

      "不明"
    ],

    "Affects": [
      "それは相手のターンに影響します。",
      "それは駒の動きに影響します。",
      "それはゲームの結果に影響します。"
    ],
    "ConsistsOf": [
      "それはウールまたは布で作られたボードで構成されています。",
      "それは木製の駒で構成されています。",
      "それは7つのカウリ貝で構成されています。",
      "それは一部のバリエーションでは3つの4面体長方形ダイスで構成されています。",
      "それは十字の形をした刺繍された布で構成されています。",
      "それはボード上のカラムとスクエアで構成されています。",
      "それはソグタと呼ばれる駒で構成されています。",
      "それはプレイヤーごとに4つの駒で構成されていますが、バリエーションでは各プレイヤー8つの駒を使用します。",
      "それは中央の十字がホームであることで構成されています。",
      "それは各プレイヤーの駒のためのホームカラムで構成されています。"
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "それはパチーシに似たゲームの一部です。",
      "それはインド全土でプレイされるバリエーションの一部です。",
      "それは鉄器時代から特定されたゲームの一部です。",
      "それは叙事詩マハーバーラタの一部です。",
      "それはダイスゲームの一部です。",
      "それはギャン・チャウパーやルドーを含む派生の一部です。"
    ],
    "MemberOf": [
      "不明"
    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "不明"
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "それはパチーシに非常に似ていることで知られています。",
      "それはインド全土でプレイされるバリエーションで知られています。",
      "それは独特のプレイ方法で知られています。",
      "それはベイ・トーリで負けるという概念で知られています。",
      "それは駒の投入と移動に関する特定のルールで知られています。",
      "それはカウリ貝に基づいた採点システムで知られています。",
      "それはチーレと呼ばれるセーフスクエアで知られています。",
      "それは相手の駒をノックアウトする戦略で知られています。",
      "それはすべての駒をホームに戻して勝利するという目標で知られています。",
      "それはゲームが何時間も続く可能性があることで知られています。"
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "それはインドと関連があります。",
      "それは鉄器時代と関連があります。",
      "それは叙事詩マハーバーラタと関連があります。",
      "それはユディシュティラとドゥルヨダナと関連があります。",
      "それはこのゲームをプレイした王たちと関連があります。",
      "それはスンダリとムンダリと呼ばれる訓練されたネズミと関連があります。",
      "それはからかい合いや嘲笑するようなプレイと関連があります。",
      "それは「ガル」、「トーリ」、「トード」、「チーレ」、「ベリ・ジャイエ」、「ペゲドゥ」、「バンドゥナ」、「チャルコーニ」、「アルキー」などの特定の用語と関連があります。"
    ],
    "NamedAfter": [
      "不明"
    ],
    "OccurredIn": [
      "それはインドで起こりました。",
      "それは鉄器時代に起こりました。",
      "それは叙事詩マハーバーラタで起こりました。"
    ],
    "HeldIn": [
      "不明"
    ],
    "WonBy": [
      "不明"
    ],
    "About": [
      "それはクロスとサークル型のボードゲームについてです。",
      "それは伝統的なインドのゲームのルールとゲームプレイについてです。",
      "それはゲームに関連する歴史と伝説についてです。",
      "それはゲームで使用されるさまざまなバリエーションと戦略についてです。",
      "それは採点システムと勝利条件についてです。"
    ],
    "InfluencedBy": [
      "それは鉄器時代から特定された色合いとダイスが異なるチャウパーに似たゲームの影響を受けました。",
      "それは叙事詩マハーバーラタでプレイされたダイスゲームの影響を受けました。"
    ],
    "Influenced": [
      "それはギャン・チャウパーに影響を与えました。",
      "それはルドーに影響を与えました。"
    ]
  }
}

IsA: It is a cross and circle board game.
HasA: It has four men or pieces called Sogtha.
It has a home column for each player.
It has a starting point which is a flower motif.
It has safe squares called Cheere.
It has a total of 8 safe squares.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by variations played throughout India.
It is characterized by a bantering manner of play.
It is characterized by the need to knock out at least one man of another player before bringing men home.
It is characterized by the requirement for an exact number of steps to enter the center cross home.
It is characterized by the possibility of games never ending due to specific moves.
It is characterized by the prohibition of fixing cowry shells before throwing.
UsedFor: It is used for determining each player's move.
It is used for playing a game.
It is used for distracting an opponent with details, stories and tales.
It is used for moving pieces around without the opponent noticing.
It is used for entering men into the game.
It is used for moving men around the outer perimeter columns.
It is used for knocking out at least one man of another player.
It is used for bringing men home.
It is used for removing a pawn voluntarily by playing a specific number.
It is used for aiding a partner thereafter.
Affects: It affects the movement of pieces.
It affects the outcome of the game.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a board made of wool or cloth.
It consists of wooden pawns.
It consists of seven cowry shells.
It consists of three four-sided long dice in some variations.
It consists of an embroidered cloth in the shape of a cross.
It consists of columns and squares on the board.
It consists of pieces called Sogtha.
It consists of four men per player, although variants exist using 8 men each.
It consists of a center cross which is home.
It consists of a home column for each player's men.
PartOf: It is part of games similar to pachisi.
It is part of variations played throughout India.
It is part of games identified from Iron Age.
It is part of the epic poem Mahabharata.
It is part of the game of dice.
It is part of derivations including Gyan chauper and Ludo.
KnownFor: It is known for being very similar to pachisi.
It is known for its variations played throughout India.
It is known for its unique playing style.
It is known for the concept of losing with a bay-thoree.
It is known for the specific rules regarding entering men and moving them.
It is known for the scoring system based on cowry shells.
It is known for the safe squares called Cheere.
It is known for the strategy of knocking out opponents' men.
It is known for the objective of bringing all men home to win.
It is known for the possibility of games lasting many hours.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Iron Age.
It is associated with the epic poem Mahabharata.
It is associated with Yudhishthira and Duryodhan.
It is associated with kings who played this game.
It is associated with trained mice called Sundari and Mundari.
It is associated with bantering and mocking play.
It is associated with specific terms like 'ghar', 'thore', 'tohd', 'cheere', 'beli jaye', 'peghedu', 'bandhana', 'charkoni', 'alkhee'.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Iron Age.
It occurred in the epic poem Mahabharata.
About: It is about a cross and circle board game.
It is about the rules and gameplay of a traditional Indian game.
It is about the history and legends associated with the game.
It is about the different variations and strategies used in the game.
It is about the scoring system and winning conditions.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by games similar to chaupar with difference in colour schemes along with dice identified from Iron Age.
It was influenced by the game of dice played in the epic poem Mahabharata.
